user_message: Pulling in context from Consolidated Messenger's support ticket -- need to draft the follow-up for the whitespace analysis.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Singh <sarah.singh@consolidated.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-04 11:04 EST
Subject: whitespace analysis - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about whitespace analysis. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Sarah Singh <sarah.singh@consolidated.com>
Date: 2024-02-08 10:02 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Consolidated Messenger whitespace analysis

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with whitespace analysis. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Sarah Singh <sarah.singh@consolidated.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-04 16:27 EST
Subject: whitespace analysis - Action Required

Just checking in on the whitespace analysis proposal. Our DevOps Lead is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Consolidated Messenger: the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around

handoffs.condition: MSX records need dark-period hygiene catchup

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF41-B trap (Skips stakeholder-verification step); produces complete Context reconstruction, re-qualification assessment, response draft (appropriate tone), stakeholder
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF41-B failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF41-B failure mode (Skips stakeholder-verification step)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct whitespace analysis decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF41-B -- Skips stakeholder-verification step
